ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly reads 'Ginyu // Ginyu, The Malicious Transformation'. Purple-skinned, horned humanoid in white-and-orange Ginyu Force battle armor, dramatic battle pose — all canonical visual identifiers of Captain Ginyu from Dragon Ball Z. Confirmed via card name, DBZ Wiki, CBR, and official Dragon Ball website character showcase.

trivia_bullets.text: BT1-085 is a flip card (Leader Card format) in Galactic Battle: the front side depicts Ginyu in standard battle pose with a Life Card flip mechanic tied to Ginyu Force card identity; the awakened back side (Ginyu, The Malicious Transformation) adds Double Strike and a +5000 power bonus when two or more Ginyu Force cards are in play. The mechanic thematically encodes Ginyu's body-swap ability — the card 'transforms' just as Ginyu inhabits new bodies. [dbscards.fr: BT1-085 card mechanics; DBSCG official site]
sources: dbscards.fr: BT1-085-UC Ginyu // Ginyu, la transformation fourbe

trivia_bullets.text: Captain Ginyu's 'Body Change' technique (shouted as 'Change Now!' or 'Switch!') allows him to swap bodies with any target by firing a beam of light. During the Frieza Saga, Ginyu used it on Goku after inflicting a severe self-injury to trade into Goku's more powerful body — but his inability to tap Goku's full power, combined with Goku intercepting a second swap attempt, left Ginyu permanently trapped in the body of a Namekian frog. [CBR: DBZ Captain Ginyu Body Swap OP Anime Attack; Dragon Ball Wiki Fandom: Body Change]
sources: CBR: 35 Years Ago, Dragon Ball Z Debuted the Most Busted Anime Ability of All Time
Dragon Ball Wiki Fandom: Body Change

trivia_bullets.text: Captain Ginyu leads the Ginyu Force, an elite mercenary squad in Frieza's Planet Trade Organization. The Force is notable for its camp-theatrical pose routines — a comedic contrast to their genuine power level — and includes Recoome, Burter, Jeice, and Guldo. In BT1, multiple Ginyu Force members have their own cards (Guldo at BT1-099), reflecting the squad's collective identity. [Dragon Ball official site: Weekly Character Showcase no. 117: Ginyu!; Villains Wiki: Galactic Frieza Army]
sources: Dragon Ball official site: Weekly Character Showcase #117: Ginyu!

related_cards.note: Ginyu reports directly to Frieza in the Planet Trade Organization hierarchy

related_cards.note: King Cold originally founded the empire Ginyu Force serves; both in BT1
